AND GREGORY LYNN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO THE THIRTEENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "TORT OF ANOTHER" IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS SCOTT AND CHRISTINE CONNORS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. HOWEVER, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THEIR OTHER CAUSES OF ACTIONS AGAINST THE DEMURRING PARTIES TO ALLEGE TORT OF ANOTHER DAMAGES DUE TO THEIR ALLEGED NEED TO BRING SUIT AGAINST DEFENDANT PAUL SEASHOLS BASED ON THE ALLEGED TORTIOUS CONDUCT OF THE DEMURRING PARTIES AND THE ABSENCE OF ANY TORTIOUS CONDUCT COMMITTED BY MR. SEASHOLS PERSONALLY. THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. THAT DOCTRINE PERMITS RECOVERY OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS AS DAMAGES IN CERTAIN LIMITED CIRCUMSTANCES. THOSE LIMITED CIRCUMSTANCES INCLUDE THE SITUATION WHERE A BUYER OF REAL PROPERTY IS REQUIRED TO INCUR ATTORNEY'S TO SUE A SELLER FOR RESCISSION OF THE SALE DUE TO THE TORTIOUS CONDUCT OF THE SELLER'S BROKER OR OTHER AGENT WHEN THE SELLER HIMSELF HAS NOT PERSONALLY COMMITTED ANY TORT. THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY AS TO ANY CLAIM WHERE A BUYER OF REAL PROPERTY SUES A SELLER OF REAL PROPERTY BASED ON ALLEGED TORTIOUS CONDUCT OF THE SELLER, WHETHER OR NOT THE BUYER ALSO ALLEGES THAT THE SELLER'S BROKER COMMITTED TORTIOUS CONDUCT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Stanley W. Smith of the law offices of Niven & Smith LLP (415-981-5451) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS RICHARD TEED, WILLIAM "BUTCH" HAZE, TEEDHAZE, and TEED HAZE DEVELOPMENT, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to the thirteenth cause of action for "tort of another" in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Scott and Christine Connors is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. However, if they can do so in good faith, plaintiffs are granted twenty days leave to amend their other causes of actions against the demurring parties to allege tort of another damages due to their alleged need to bring suit against defendant Paul Seashols based on the alleged tortious conduct of the demurring parties and the absence of any tortious conduct committed by Mr. Seashols personally. The tort of another doctrine is not a cause of action. That doctrine permits recovery of attorney's fees and costs as damages in certain limited circumstances. Those limited circumstances include the situation where a buyer of real property is required to incur attorney's to sue a seller for rescission of the sale due to the tortious conduct of the seller's broker or other agent when the seller himself has not personally committed any tort. The tort of another doctrine does not apply as to any claim where a buyer of real property sues a seller of real property based on alleged tortious conduct of the seller, whether or not the buyer also alleges that the seller's broker committed tortious conduct. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
